steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS W/O CONTRAST)

study_inadequacy.evidence: Evaluation of solid abdominal viscera is limited by lack of IV contrast. ... Vessel patency cannot be examined in this evaluation.
study_inadequacy.note: CT was performed without IV contrast due to thyrotoxicosis. The report explicitly states that solid organ evaluation and vascular patency are limited. Additionally, sigmoid diverticula with surrounding fluid and stranding are noted, raising concern for diverticulitis, but the absence of IV contrast limits full characterization of inflammatory change and rules out vascular complications.

alarm_detect.summary: The CT abdomen/pelvis was performed without IV contrast (explicitly noted as limiting solid organ and vascular evaluation), leaving the full extent of the sigmoid diverticular inflammation and any vascular or solid-organ pathology incompletely characterized — an inadequate study for a complete abdominal workup.

alarm_resolve.reason: Pelvic ultrasound can characterize gynecologic structures and pelvic free fluid without contrast, but it does not address the primary inadequacy flagged — the limited evaluation of solid abdominal viscera, vascular patency, and full characterization of the sigmoid diverticular inflammation that resulted from the absence of IV contrast on CT.
